description: Review the general features of the lymphatic system.

cards.text: What are the four main functions of the lymphatic system?
cards.answer: Fluid homeostasis, lipid absorption, immune surveillance, waste removal

cards.text: Where does the thoracic duct terminate?

cards.answer: Specialised lymphatic capillaries in intestinal villi that absorb dietary fats

tip.tip: The milky fluid they carry is called chyle

cards.text: What structural feature allows lymphatic capillaries to absorb large molecules?
cards.answer: Overlapping endothelial cells with no tight junctions and absent basement membrane

tip.tip: Unlike blood capillaries which have continuous basement membranes

cards.text: What is a sentinel lymph node?
cards.answer: The first lymph node draining a tumour

tip.tip: Identified by sentinel lymph node biopsy in cancer staging

cards.text: Name two causes of secondary lymphoedema.
cards.answer: Post-surgical lymph node dissection and filariasis (Wuchereria bancrofti)

cards.text: What is the difference between primary and secondary lymphoedema?
cards.answer: Primary is congenital (e.g. Milroy disease); secondary is acquired (e.g. post-surgical, filariasis)

cards.text: What are anchoring filaments in lymphatic capillaries?
cards.answer: Elastic fibres that anchor endothelial cells to surrounding matrix, pulling vessels open during tissue swelling

tip.tip: They ensure lymphatics open when needed most

cards.answer: Jugular, subclavian, bronchomediastinal, intercostal, lumbar, intestinal

tip.tip: Named after the body regions they drain

cards.text: Which lymphoid organs are part of MALT?

tip.tip: Found in the respiratory and gastrointestinal tracts
